data.title: Buy B2Gold: The Bullish Rally Is Just Beginning
data.description:
Investment thesis

The current environment in the gold market remains highly positive, with prices hitting new all-time highs. This week, we saw gold reach $3,647 per ounce, a new record that shows no signs of slowing down and is poised to climb higher. The price surge is driven by a combination of factors, including mounting global and U.S. recession fears and expectations of increased liquidity in financial markets. A price of $4,000 per ounce is no longer a prospect for 2026 but is now a potential target by the end of 2025. Accordingly, these developments have created a strong bullish trend for gold mining stocks. B2Gold (NYSE:BTG), in particular, stands out with a high Quant Rating of 4.92 points. While the company ranks ninth out of 47 in the gold mining sector, its impressive and diversified asset portfolio enables it to generate a stable operating cash flow. The primary investment case for BTG shares is tied to its new projects, which are expected to significantly boost gold production over the next 9-13 years. The launch of new mines at Gramalote and the Goose Mine alone is projected to increase gold output by 537,000 ounces from 2026 to 2031, based on expert estimates. This represents a substantial 66.7% increase over the company's total 2024 production.

Company overview

A major Canadian gold mining company, B2Gold, operates mines in Mali, Namibia, and the Philippines, in addition to its Canadian assets. The company is also actively engaged in gold exploration in other parts of the world. B2Gold's presence on four continents diversifies its operations, covering everything from geological exploration to gold extraction. The company also implements numerous projects for the development and exploration of deposits in various countries. In 2024, the company's total gold production was 804 thousand ounces. In 2025, gold production is projected to increase to between 970 thousand and 1.075 million ounces. Factors that are expected to drive the increase in gold production this year include the operation of new mines and the processing of gold ore with a higher gold content, which reflects an improvement in production efficiency.

Among the company's new projects is the Gramalote gold mine in Colombia, which requires an estimated $740 million in capital. The project's economic viability is underpinned by a projected internal rate of return [IRR] of 33.5% at the current spot gold price of $3,300 per ounce. It is expected to produce 2.3 million ounces of gold over its 13-year lifespan. Annual gold production in the first 5 years will be 227 thousand ounces, and in the following years—177 thousand ounces. The economic sensitivity of the Gramalote gold mining project in Colombia is shown in the table below. Taking into account the forecasts for the growth of the gold price, the probability of obtaining maximum benefit from the implementation of the project has increased significantly.

Another project that will be delivered in Q2 2025 is the Goose Mine. The mine has a mine life of 9 years. However, the average annual gold production over a 5-year period will be 310 thousand ounces. The estimated mineral resources at this mine are estimated at 2,440,000 ounces of gold (10.1 million tonnes at 7.54 g/t gold). Based on the updated mineral resource information for the Goose project, coupled with exploration prospects in the Back River gold deposit area, B2Gold has initiated a study to analyze the possibility of increasing the plant capacity from 4,000 tonnes per day to 6,000 tonnes per day. The average annual production at these mines over a 9-year mine life will be 270 thousand ounces.

The company is also working productively in cooperation with the Malian authorities. On 30 July 2025, the Malian authorities granted B2Gold a permit to commence underground mining at the Fekola mine. This project includes over 9,300 meters of development work and the installation of all necessary underground infrastructure. Underground mining at Fekola remains an important component of the company’s near-term production profile. Underground production at Fekola is expected to be between 25,000 and 35,000 ounces of gold in 2025, accelerating significantly in 2026 and beyond.

B2Gold’s strong and profitable production profile is reflected in production and price dynamics. The company expects production volumes to reach high levels in 2025.

Production through the first two quarters of 2025 has already reached 422,206 ounces of gold. Fekola has produced 220,166 ounces of gold, Masbate has produced 97,107 ounces, Otjikoto has produced 104,241 ounces, and the new Goose has so far produced only 692 ounces of gold. Despite Goose’s small share, it is the start of a major new project for the company and demonstrates strong prospects for gold production volumes for the period 2026-2031.

B2Gold continues to estimate gold production in 2025 to be between 120,000 and 150,000 ounces, with average annual gold production over the first six full years of operation (2026-2031 inclusive) of approximately 300,000 ounces per year, based on the existing Goose mineral reserves alone. The only problem is that the operating costs at its mines are $1,520 to $1,580, compared to $635 to $910 at other mines. However, this estimate should change in the coming years as Goose’s operational processes mature. As gold production scales up, fixed costs will decrease relative to the overall cost of an ounce of gold, improving the mine’s economics.

B2Gold Financial Profile: Buy Signal

Valuation Grade and Underlying Metrics are a key indicator of B2Gold's investment appeal. The company's non-GAAP forward P/E is 7.44, significantly lower than the sector median of 16.22. P/E GAAP [FWD] is 8.26, which is also significantly better than the sector average of 18.41. It is also worth paying attention to EV/EBITDA (TTM), which is 5.52, which is also better than the sector median of 10.07.

Growth Grade and Underlying Metrics also characterize B2Gold as a financially stable player in the gold mining sector, which has investment-attractive shares. Detailed values ​​of these indicators are shown below. The only concerns are with the company's cash flows, which are predictably strained by its large-scale capital investments in new projects. The goal of these investments is to develop new deposits and drive growth in both gold production and overall operational efficiency. Therefore, the negative free cash flow is not a risk to the company's financial stability.

According to the latest quarterly reporting for Q2 2025, B2Gold's gold production was 229,454 ounces. Consolidated cash operating expenses were $745 per ounce of gold mined. Consolidated all-in sustaining costs were $1,519 per ounce of gold sold. Attributable net income was $0.12 per share; adjusted attributable net income was similarly $0.12 per share. More detailed financial results of B2Gold for Q2 2025 are presented below.

B2Gold risks exist, but their probability is low

One of B2Gold's key risks is the Goose Mine project in Mali. While the company has cultivated productive relationships with Malian authorities, they cannot be considered reliable. This is due to the political crisis in the country, making it an unfavorable region for long-term investments. The Malian government has a history of seizing gold mines, which disrupts production and creates significant uncertainty for future operations.

Other risks are associated with the price factor in the gold market. The ongoing bullish trend may end, which will cause gold quotes to go down. This action will force B2Gold to revise its financial forecasts and look for ways to optimize operating costs in gold mining. However, this risk seems unlikely to me. There is no reason to expect a decrease in the price of gold. In the event of a recession in the economy, the quotes of the precious metal will go up, because it is a safety asset. In addition, gold is growing on increasing inflation risks. But even if inflation declines and the Fed eases its monetary policy, the liquidity of financial markets will increase. Gold is a popular asset for savings and investments, which is why increasing liquidity will increase its value.

BTG Stock Forecast and Targets

Forecasting a target price for BTG shares using a DCF model is challenging due to the company's negative free cash flow [FCF]. B2Gold FCF was -$33 million in 2024 and -$96 million in 2023. However, these figures are not a cause for concern, as they are a result of the company's high investment spending. Operating cash flow for 2024 was a robust $877.6 million, and the negative FCF is directly attributable to the significant capital investments in new projects like the Goose and Fekola mines.

The key tool for forecasting the target price of B2Gold shares is the discount rate. Finbox financial resource calculations estimate the company's WACC at 9.3%, using B2Gold's cost of equity. The expected growth of B2Gold profits in the period 2026-2030 is 15%. Such a high expected growth is due to the influence of several expected factors at once. The first category of triggers is associated with an increase in gold production at new deposits. For example, the expansion of mines at Fekola from 2026 to 2030 will be able to bring in an additional 180,000 ounces of gold. The second important factor in forecasting B2Gold profit growth by 15% annually is the continuation of the bullish trend in the gold market. Consensus estimates of financial analysts boil down to the fact that by 2030 the price of gold will reach a record high of $5,000.

In order to predict the growth of BTG shares, it is recommended to refer to the technical analysis of the asset price movement chart. First, I suggest looking at the instrument from the standpoint of classical technical analysis, using trend lines and support/resistance levels on a weekly time frame.

On the chart, it is worth paying attention to the key support level—2.16. It is formed at the minimum level for March 2020. The formation of the ascending trend channel ended with a price breakout at the end of August 2025. Consolidation signals an acceleration of the bullish trend, which means an improvement in the growth forecasts for BTG shares. The first target is the resistance level of 4.40, which is the maximum of April 2023. A breakout of this level will allow prices to rise to the next resistance level of 5.07. The maximum of April 2022 is located there. If the bullish trend for BTG shares continues, reaching the target level of 5.07 from current prices will give a price increase of 16.82%. The time period for achieving this goal may be one or two quarters.

I also suggest looking at BTG shares from the side of the "Smart Money" concept, the main principle of which is to work with liquidity. For this, I will also refer to the weekly chart.

We have a bullish order flow formation, signaling an upward trend. Initially, liquidity was worked at the level of 2.35. This withdrawal formed another liquidity work at the level of 2.56. After that, the price formed two price inefficiency zones in the form of weekly bullish imbalances [FVG], which were successfully tested by the chart. This allowed us to get 2 new confirmations of the bullish order flow, continuing the growth of the shares. The target targets for BTG shares are the higher levels at which the liquidity withdrawal should occur. These are the same levels of 4.40 and 5.07.

Conclusion

In conclusion, my fundamental analysis of BTG shares indicates a high degree of investment attractiveness. I have a target price of $5.07, but I believe the stock's growth is not limited to this level. Given the ongoing bullish trend in the gold market, which is expected to continue for the foreseeable future, B2Gold shares have the potential to return to their historical high of $7.50, reached in the summer of 2020. The company is actively raising its production forecasts by bringing new mines at Gramalote and Goose Mine online. Since these projects are just beginning to ramp up, their full economic impact is expected to be realized in the second half of 2025 and beyond. My recommendation for BTG shares is "Buy," with the potential to upgrade to "Strong Buy."

data.title: Oracle: Keep The Position As AI Pushes Forward (Rating Downgrade)
data.description:
On September 24th of 2024, I published an article about Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) with a Buy rating. Since then, and despite a fear related to the firm’s valuation, the company has presented a total return of more than 90% (including the rally after the latest quarterly report), more than six times higher than the performance of the S&P 500. This article will update Oracle’s finances and thesis. The company continues to present positive financials and prospects and is suitable for the continuation of allocation in equity portfolios. The new rating is Hold.

AI Investment Thesis

Since last year, enterprise spending related to AI has become a reality. More and more firms are developing tools aimed at creating new products, improving operating efficiency, and improving sales capabilities. Currently, a great majority of companies invest in AI, but only 1% believe it is at maturity to generate proper returns. Investment linked to the technology is viewed as a must-do by organizations preparing to face future competition.

Moreover, in a previous article I claimed that AI was changing from current pure language models to a reasoning system that will require an additional level of computing but unveil sharper use cases and agentic environments. Another important point was related to the fact that AI was shifting from an “only” hyperscale business to a wider investment thesis including broader application developments. This environment is exactly the one pushing enterprise enablers such as Oracle. AI IT spending is estimated to grow at a 31.9% CAGR until 2029 and achieve US$ 1.3 trillion. The engines of this move are related to the agentic AI, with more applicable solutions but requiring stronger computing capabilities and more complex software landscapes. The vision is that AI will not be a standalone solution that provides input to current informational solutions but will rather be integrated into the incumbent software and decision-making of firms.

Oracle is positioned as a twofold enterprise solution provider. On the one hand, it delivers a robust cloud environment that may allow clients to engage in AI model development, training, and operation in a high-capacity, cost-effective manner. On the other hand, the firm's expertise in ERPs resulted in the constitution of tailored, pre-trained models for many industries, allowing for lower-risk, ready-to-market profiles for investing customers. In this way, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is a powerful tool for enhancing value to customers that allocate significant capital into AI with a certain degree of uncertainty in terms of return. Since the last quarter, new developments related to multi-cloud integration and AI readiness were observed, with enhanced programming features, analytics and data lakes, and the expansion of AI agents in the portfolio. Computing capacity was expanded with an aggressive CapEx plan that includes the development of new cloud regions, new superclusters using mainly Nvidia, stronger interconnection, and an R&D center. Another important point is that Oracle operates its data centers in more regions than any competitor, resulting in a solid market position for sovereign clouds, which became a crucial requirement in times of geopolitical separations. This unique positioning resulted in Oracle being recognized as a leader in the Gartner magic quadrant for the third consecutive year.

Commercially speaking, the recent announcement unveiled an astonishing backlog increase to almost half a trillion in a situation where “demand continues to dramatically outstrip supply”. In another development, the deal closed with OpenAI for cloud infrastructure is one of the largest in history. This segment is expected to grow 77% to US$ 18 billion this year and expand eight times in the next four years.

In line with this development, Mr. Ellison’s statement in the earnings call closes the argument I’ve been discussing. It centers on the fact that Oracle is positioned on the new wave of AI, inferencing. As this market is potentially larger than current AI training, it will leverage existing data stored in the customers, and Oracle is a lead custodian of this data, allowing it to be able to turn AI applications faster and more reliably and adding value to clients.

Oracle Segments

ORCL is a traditional player in the ERP and management software industry and now combines its strength with a robust AI offering focusing on application development. The business focuses on three segments.

  • Cloud and License: comprises subscription-based business for software applications and infrastructure technologies, recognized along contractual terms. It represents the great majority of businesses, around 86.5% of the total (1.5 p.p. higher in the YoY comparison), growing 13% YoY with a 59.5% Gross Margin.
  • Hardware: the company offers specific hardware solutions, such as servers and storage units, recognized at the point of ownership transfer. This competitive business accounts for less than 5% of the business, flat YoY, with a 67% margin.
  • Services: cloud and license businesses are complemented by a service offering, covering activities such as consulting and support, to maximize customer utility in usage of Oracle’s solutions. This segment represents around 7% of the business, with a 6% YoY increase and a Gross Margin of 24%.

Latest Financials

When analyzing Oracle, it is important to consider the three main trends involved in its performance. The first is related to the robust growth prospect, not as much in terms of the current numbers reported but in terms of the impact that the huge backlog will have on the financial future of the firm. Another point is related to the strong CapEx program undertaken by the company to be able to materialize this growth, which drains short-term capital. Finally, it is important to consider the impact of the restructuring programs going on in the firm to unlock capital to bridge the short-term capital need from Capex and the future revenue and cash conversion. As Figure 1 depicts, the evaluation of the top-line and margin development highlights some important aspects. Initially, there is a clear positive trend in the revenue during the last five years. This trend is, as explained, expected to continue as a result of the latest commercial successes that Oracle achieved, resulting in a strong war chest, i.e., backlog. The company also depicts a certain seasonality with sales concentrated in the last quarter of the firm’s fiscal year, which in this case is May.

Personal Note: I have seen many customers in large and long contracts pushing for deliveries at the end of fiscal years in order to avoid problems related to Capex carry-over. The great majority of the firms, on the other hand, don’t close the fiscal year in May, so it may imply that revenue recognition on closing year is a point of analysis.

Additionally, this quarter depicted a gross margin around 61%, which is lower than the almost constant trend the company has recorded since the incorporation of Cerner in 2022. This effect comes directly from the margin reduction in Cloud and Software, which consists of personnel and infrastructure costs. This can be linked to the augmented infrastructure that the CapEx program is undergoing as well as geographic distribution. In this case, one should expect and track that gross margins return to normality as billing from backlog becomes a reality. In terms of the bottom line, Oracle was able to mitigate at least part of the gross margin reduction on net income, linked to lower amortization of intangibles. Figure 2 illustrates Oracle’s margin development.

In my research, I compare several target firms that are selected based on an appropriate investment thesis. Right now, some of the relevant theses include Artificial Intelligence Hardware and Applications, Infrastructure and Cybersecurity, defense, among others. Some comparisons in this article will be made against this broad group, and some will be made with the direct competitor in the AI Enterprise Application segment. In this case, Oracle is compared against a firm that is more directly focused on AI enterprise applications, Palantir (PLTR). In general, firms are compared in terms of four elements. The first is related to the Cash Discipline trying to evaluate how well the operation converts cash. The second is related to the total yield an investor receives considering the payout and the potential reinvestment of cash at current prices. The third category is growth, to understand commercial success and how it translates into real profitability growth. The last one is a measure of momentum, as it is a strong performance indicator and also may impose an important demand engine due to the amount of passive investments.

The sales growth and margin comparison with Palantir impose two conclusions. The first is that the competitor has been more successful in driving growth and gross margin in recent periods. Figure 3 depicts that in both 1- and 3-year time frames, growth in Palantir is at least three times that of Oracle. Sure, Oracle is a company in a completely different maturity stage than Palantir, with more than 15x more revenues and established in the ERP business. Considering the already hired growth, Oracle depicts an impressive performance. The second point is related to scale; despite the advantage in gross margins, both Oracle and Palantir are in the same Net Income margin, with Palantir increasing from its initial phase and consolidating in the positive field since 2023. Figure 4 depicts the margin comparisons between the two players. Oracle recorded US$ 402 million out of a US$ 1.6 billion in restructuring costs planned to occur in this fiscal year. The company does not disclose results at this point, and future tracking is important.

In terms of the balance sheet, the firms experienced a decrease in liquidity related to the use of cash, mostly for their relevant CapEx program. At the current levels, the current ratio is at a warning level. Since the company did not engage in additional acquisitions, goodwill remained flat and reduced when compared to sales. The debt level, on the other hand, increased 18% following CapEx pressure. The increase is mainly attributed to lease contracts. When compared to equity, debt/equity suffered major fluctuations related to the fact that equity was negative in part of the analyzed period. Right now, a level of more than 4 also highlights a warning in terms of debt level. Figure 5 shows these balance sheet indicators.

As a result, Oracle is struggling to be able to compensate investors in a 4-year analysis that scales the numbers by the last sales figures. Despite being able to generate consistent operating cash, as illustrated by Figure 6, mostly related to sales activities, some consistent results, ex-May, were recorded as well. On Figure 7, CapEx consumes around 70% of cash generated by operations, which, in tandem with other acquisitions, eliminates FCF. In this way, investor payout has been majorly funded by debt. Dividend is stable in each fiscal year, but its growth and yield are classified as D+ and F.

In the general comparison, the firm presents a risk profile. As depicted by Figure 8, true yield and debt levels are in a concerning scenario. Figure 9 depicts that the PEG ratio is currently at a high level, indicating some stress in the current valuation when compared to past growth levels. Looking forward, the company needs to perform well to return to healthier levels. At last, the company is evidently well graded in momentum, mostly after the report. For new investors, it is important to use the right tactics on new positions, as corrections are a normal feature of price discovery. From a longer perspective, momentum, as an important indicator, may potentially command further price movements. Figure 10 illustrates some momentum indicators.

Main Risks of the Position

Despite the strong price movement, the previous session explained that the company has some major challenges that must be tracked by investors:

  • Revenue Growth: ORCL has not yet been able to capture stellar growth. As the backlog is consistent, it seems that this risk is well under control.
  • Capex Level: Oracle currently commands a relevant Capex program. This impacted several numbers in the firm’s financials, and its proper execution is a concerning point.
  • Restructuring Costs: ORCL is engaged continuously in restructuring plans to ensure operational fitness. Eventually, one should expect margin improvements as a result of such actions.
  • Debt Level: ORCL's debt level is a potential source of volatility. In January, Fitch reaffirmed the BBB (Stable) credit rating to the company. In June, the rating was reiterated. The agency expects leverage stabilization, good debt maturity management, and strong liquidity for its decision.

Conclusion

After a year with a Buy rating, I’m reducing the rating to Hold. Investors must understand the rating from a portfolio perspective. After the strong year, Oracle still seems suitable to be considered in equity portfolios, keeping the position previously established, which generated consistent gains. Some of the financial risks impose some burden on the general scenario, but further news indicating development on the financials may result in new rating reviews. Tactical and long-term investors also must understand momentum as a positive tailwind in the long-term perspective.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: FedEx, Lennar To Report Earnings As Investors Await Fed Rate Decision
data.description:
Get ahead of the market by subscribing to Seeking Alpha's Wall Street Week Ahead, a preview of key events scheduled for the coming week. The newsletter keeps you informed of the biggest stories set to make headlines, including upcoming IPOs, investor days, earnings reports, and conference presentations.

Stock index futures were down on Friday, a day after Wall Street notched up record highs, with rate cut bets firmly in place now. U.S. stocks set another record closing on Thursday, with the Dow Jones reaching the 46,000 level for the first time, after the consumer inflation report came in higher on a monthly basis in August, while core CPI was steady. As the week comes to a close, markets are largely in a buoyant mood, continuing into the morning's Asian session. Investor attention was squarely focused on the slightly higher than expected CPI release and the notably higher than expected jobless claims data.

Let's take a look at some important economic data due to be released next week. This includes the Empire State Manufacturing Index on Monday, with reports on Retail Sales, Import Price Index, and Export Price Index to be released on Tuesday. Wednesday will see the release of MBA Mortgage Applications, Housing Starts, and Building Permits, along with the Fed interest rate decision. While Thursday will see the release of Initial Jobless Claims and the Fed's Balance Sheet, with Baker Hughes Rig Count on Friday.

FedEx (FDX), Lennar (LEN), FactSet Research (FDS), Ferguson (FERG), Evolution Petroleum (EPM), Flux Power Holdings (FLUX), Dave & Buster's Entertainment (PLAY), and Radiant (RLGT) will report earnings next week.

FedEx Earnings Preview: FedEx is set to report fiscal first quarter earnings next week, with analysts expecting the company to report an adjusted profit of $3.67 in the fiscal first quarter on $21.71B in revenue. Despite the negative view of FQ1 results, J.P. Morgan's Ossenbeck sees upside in the stock's valuation warranted by the Freight spin-off and long-term potential in combining the Ground and Express networks.
